page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features a dedicated Intel processor with 1 physical core and 2 threads per core. The CPU configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated vector and matrix operations, alongside cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the instance delivers average single-core capabilities, as demonstrated by a stress-ng single-core score of 1684.29 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1834.0. However, multi-core and memory subsystem performance is constrained. The stress-ng multi-core score of 1714.68 and Geekbench multi-core score of 2251.0 are categorized as poor and weak respectively, reflecting the single physical core limitation. Memory bandwidth is also low, with large-block read and write speeds registering at 19.35 GB/sec and 12.78 GB/sec.

This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-standard-2 provides a standard resource density of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a budget-conscious option for workloads that do not require high parallel processing power or high memory throughput.

Appropriate workloads for the n4-standard-2 include lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and low-traffic static web serving, which achieved a throughput of 4.0 GB/sec in testing. It is less suitable for database operations, software compilation, or multi-threaded tasks due to its weak multi-core and memory bandwidth profiles.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM, backed by a dedicated Intel processor with 1 physical core and 2 threads. The CPU includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark results show average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1834.0, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weak to poor due to the single physical core design. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. It is qualitatively positioned as a cost-efficient option for low-resource, single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, and lightweight static web serving, while being less optimal for memory-intensive or highly parallel workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a dedicated Intel CPU. It offers average single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is a general-purpose VM with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and dedicated Intel hardware for basic single-threaded workloads.
tagline: An entry-level general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processing, advanced matrix extensions, and a balanced memory-to-core ratio for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM on a dedicated Intel x86_64 processor
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Average single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and static web serving
